_comment: Source of truth for project status. Don't hand-edit `parts`/`scenario` here directly -- use `python3 tools/sync_status.py set-part ...` / `set-scenario ...`, which updates this file AND regenerates the matching section of PROJECT.md so the two can't drift. If you do hand-edit this file, a hook regenerates PROJECT.md automatically on save.

infra_scaffold.notes: Generic placeholder operations, not scenario-specific yet

scenario_selection.notes: Olympic Games Management System: venues/matches/ticketing/volunteers/shuttles/restaurants/subscriptions/country scores, 9 base ops + go_live (Part 3)

part1_traditional.notes: Skeleton exists (Traditional/), needs real ops + a couple runs of the HTTP server mode to demo it's a "real" server

part3_feature_extension.notes: Feature chosen: go_live cross-cutting cascade (fan-out + live standings, pure Python). Plus project_medals parallel-throughput benchmark for the FaaS-win axis. C++ accelerator dropped.

part4_performance.notes: Design: perf on base workload + state-growth scaling + two concurrency benchmarks (seat-race, parallel-throughput). Runs on Linux/matanco.space (Windows dev box lacks perf).

part5_security_maintainability.notes: Team decided to skip (optional per course amendment); effort focused on Parts 1-4 instead

report.notes: report/report.typ + report/ids.typ skeletons exist (Typst, not installed in this sandbox)

blockers: perf not installed in dev sandbox (needed for part4_performance)
typst not installed in dev sandbox (needed for report compile)
scenario not chosen (blocks renaming common/operations.py's 7 placeholder ops)
